title: Agentic AI Tools Best Practice
summary: Explains best-in-class tool use in agentic systems through tool selection, contracts, permissions, observability, verification, and fallback behavior.
claims: Best-in-class agentic tool use depends on selection, contracts, permission boundaries, observability, verification, and safe fallback behavior.
The number of tools matters less than whether tools are governed and trusted in execution.
Tool use becomes enterprise-capable when every meaningful action is explicit, logged, and checked.
proof_points: This record complements the existing Joz architecture capability by making tool execution quality explicit.
It matches the backend direction of tool routing, traceability, and validation rather than blind autonomy.
It provides a safer explanation of tool use than generic agent demos or prompt-only positioning.
